title: Ruyschiana thymiflora
intro: Ruyschiana thymiflora, also known as Thymelaea hirsuta or Thymelaea hirsuta var. thymiflora, is a perennial shrub native to Europe and Africa. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are thought to aid in soothing respiratory issues, reducing stress, and protecting against oxidative damage. The bioactive constituents of Ruyschiana thymiflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Ruyschiana thymiflora is commonly used to prepare herbal teas and infusions, as well as tinctures and extracts, which are traditionally used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and asthma.
taxonomy: Ruyschiana thymiflora is classified as a genus within the family Brassicaceae. This flowering plant belongs to the kingdom Plantae and is a type of herb. Ruyschiana thymiflora is native to the Mediterranean region and is often found in dry areas and rocky outcrops. The species is characterized by its small yellow flowers and has been used in traditional medicine. Ruyschiana thymiflora is also known by its synonym, Erysimum ruyschianum.
